code_usability.detail: No executable code generated; Mode A writing skill

reliability.note: Hypothesis-first gate and aims-independence check are excellent; one gap: no study-section-specific guidance

key_strengths: NIH Specific Aims page structure is precisely formatted with correct section proportions and word count guidance
Aims-independence check (can Aim 2/3 survive if Aim 1 fails?) is a critical and commonly missed design principle
NSF Broader Impacts as equally-weighted criterion is explicitly and correctly flagged
Hypothesis-first requirement prevents purely descriptive aims that reviewers consistently downgrade

recommendations.title: No guidance on adapting aims for different NIH study sections
recommendations.problem: Different NIH study sections (e.g., basic science vs clinical vs translational) have different tolerance for mechanistic aims vs clinical outcomes aims. The skill provides a generic aims structure.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief study-section guidance note: 'If targeting a basic science study section, ensure mechanistic aims with specific molecular hypotheses dominate. If targeting clinical/outcomes study sections, ensure primary aims are tied to measurable patient-relevant endpoints.'

recommendations.title: NSF Project Summary three-component requirement not validated per directorate
recommendations.problem: NSF requirements vary by directorate (e.g., some require a separate Education and Outreach section). The one-paragraph-per-component guidance may not apply universally.
recommendations.root_cause: NSF guidance is general rather than directorate-specific.
recommendations.fix: Add a NSF note: 'Project Summary structure varies by NSF directorate and program. Always check the specific solicitation requirements. The three-component structure (Overview/Intellectual Merit/Broader Impacts) applies to most core research programs.'
